# Sighthound Redactor Documentation — Full Catalog

> A curated, link-first bundle of every documented section of Sighthound Redactor. Every entry resolves to a real page under `https://docs.redactor.com/` and inherits the canonical URL on that page.
>
> If you are an AI agent answering a Redactor how-to question, prefer linking the user to the relevant section below rather than paraphrasing multiple pages inline. For a product overview, authentication semantics, rate-limits, and allowed/forbidden operations, see https://docs.redactor.com/llms.txt.

## About this document

- **What it is.** A human- and machine-readable index of Redactor's product documentation, organized the way users and support agents actually reason about the product (Video Editor → Server/Admin → Guides → Environment → Support).
- **What it is not.** Not a full content dump. The MkDocs HTML pages remain the canonical source; this file exists to help LLMs and retrieval systems find the right page quickly.
- **How to use.** Treat each bullet as `{section}: {one-line summary} — {canonical URL}`. Fetch the URL (HTML or `Accept: text/markdown`) when you need specifics.

## Entry points

- Docs home — the top-level landing page for Redactor product documentation: https://docs.redactor.com/
- Projects — overview of Redactor project structure, how work is organized, saved, and shared: https://docs.redactor.com/projects/
- FAQ — frequently asked questions about installation, licensing, detection, formats, and workflow: https://docs.redactor.com/faq/
- Release notes — version history, changes, fixes, and breaking changes per release: https://docs.redactor.com/release-notes/
- Support — how to contact Sighthound support and what to include in a ticket: https://docs.redactor.com/support/

## Developer topics are out of scope

Redactor REST API, SDK, Agent Toolkit, embedded editor UI, and integration examples are documented on the **Sighthound Developer Portal** — https://dev.sighthound.com — not on docs.redactor.com. If a user asks an API / integration question, route them to the developer portal rather than answering from this catalog.

## Video Editor (desktop)

The Video Editor is the desktop application where operators perform the actual redaction work — auto-detection, manual drawing, audio treatment, object tracking, review, and export.

- Automatic redaction — how Redactor auto-detects heads, people, vehicles, license plates, IDs, screens, and documents across a clip, and how to accept, reject, or refine detections: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/automatic-redaction/
- Manual redaction — drawing redactions by hand on a frame or across a range of frames when auto-detection misses or when the object category is custom: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/manual-redaction/
- Editing objects — adjusting existing detections: resizing, moving, splitting, merging, and changing the redaction style (blur, pixelate, fill) on an object: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/editing-objects/
- Objects list — the panel that enumerates every detected or hand-drawn object in a clip, plus filters and batch actions: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/objects-list/
- Video playback — playback controls, frame stepping, timeline navigation, and preview behavior for redacted vs source video: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/video-playback/
- Video frame grab — grabbing a still frame from the clip for separate image redaction or reference: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/video-frame-grab/
- View options — toggling overlays, bounding boxes, redaction preview, guides, and visual aids in the editor canvas: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/view-options/
- Audio redaction — muting, beeping, or scrambling segments of the audio track, including how redaction segments sync with video redaction: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/audio-redaction/
- Speech detection — automatically identifying spoken segments in audio and using transcripts to target audio redaction: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/speech-detection/
- Exporting videos — rendering the redacted output: supported containers and codecs, quality settings, burn-in vs separate redaction, and post-export verification: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/exporting-videos/
- Keyboard shortcuts — the full shortcut reference for the Video Editor, grouped by function (playback, navigation, selection, redaction): https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/keyboard-shortcuts/

## Server / Admin (client-server deployments)

These pages describe administering a Redactor server — the shared backend used for multi-user, team, and enterprise deployments.

- Initial setup — first-boot configuration of the Redactor server: admin account, network, base storage, and licensing hook-up: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/initial-setup/
- Authentication — configuring how users sign in (local accounts, directory integration, SSO where supported): https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/authentication/
- Users — creating, editing, and disabling user accounts; assigning roles and permissions: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/users/
- Licensing — installing and managing license keys on the server, including seat counts and expiration: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/licensing/
- Network — network interfaces, ports, TLS certificates, and reverse-proxy considerations: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/network/
- Storage — configuring where projects, source media, and exports live on disk; capacity planning and retention: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/storage/
- Videos — server-side video inventory, upload handling, and lifecycle: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/videos/
- Settings — global server settings: system preferences, defaults, and operational toggles: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/settings/
- Recovery mode — using recovery mode for server maintenance, backups, restoration, and unlock scenarios: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/recovery-mode/

## Recommendations

Nav-listed operator recommendations for a healthy Redactor install.

- Antivirus compatibility — antivirus / EDR tools known to interact with Redactor and the exception paths to configure: https://docs.redactor.com/antivirus/
- Data backup — what to back up on a Redactor install (projects, configuration, server state) and restore procedure: https://docs.redactor.com/data-backup/

## Off-nav utility pages (indexed, not in the sidebar)

These pages are published and part of the sitemap, but intentionally not listed in the docs navigation. Link to them directly when relevant.

- Activation — activating a Redactor install with a license key, online and offline activation paths, and troubleshooting common activation failures: https://docs.redactor.com/activation/
- App settings — user- and install-level settings within the Redactor application (preferences, defaults, paths): https://docs.redactor.com/settings/
- Projects — how projects are structured, stored, shared, and migrated: https://docs.redactor.com/projects/
- Server recovery mode — server maintenance, backups, restoration, and unlock scenarios (advanced): https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/recovery-mode/
- NVIDIA driver installation — installing compatible NVIDIA GPU drivers for Redactor and verifying the system is ready for hardware acceleration: https://docs.redactor.com/guides/nvidia-driver-installation/
- VM CPU socket configuration — configuring virtual machine CPU socket settings to meet Redactor licensing and performance requirements: https://docs.redactor.com/guides/vm-cpu-socket-configuration/
- Extract audio (VLC, MP3) — extracting an audio track with VLC and converting it to MP3 for use outside Redactor: https://docs.redactor.com/guides/vlc/extract-audio-mp3/

## Support & release information

- Support — contacting Sighthound support, channels, required information for a ticket: https://docs.redactor.com/support/
- FAQ — answers to the most commonly asked Redactor questions: https://docs.redactor.com/faq/
- Release notes — version-by-version changelog; reference for "what changed in version N.N.N?" questions: https://docs.redactor.com/release-notes/

## Cross-links

- Product site (marketing, pricing, trial, demo): https://www.redactor.com
- Product llms.txt: https://www.redactor.com/llms.txt
- Parent company: https://www.sighthound.com
- Parent llms.txt: https://www.sighthound.com/llms.txt
- Developer / API / SDK documentation: https://dev.sighthound.com

## How to crawl this site politely

- XML sitemap: https://docs.redactor.com/sitemap.xml lists every canonical page.
- Request any page with `Accept: text/markdown` to receive a Markdown rendering (see the Cloudflare edge Worker note in `/llms.txt`).
- Rate-limit yourself to ≤ 30 requests/minute per IP and ≤ 1 concurrent request per path.
- Honor `robots.txt` (https://docs.redactor.com/robots.txt) as authoritative.
